---
title: "OUR TITLE HERE"
subtitle: "RIKEN Wako Science Camp 2026"
author: "Student A, Student B, Student C, Student D"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default]
    slide-number: true
    embed-resources: true
    transition: slide
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

<!--
HOW TO USE THIS TEMPLATE
1. Save a copy as  slides/our_presentation.qmd
2. Replace the title and author line above
3. Fill in each slide (delete our hint comments)
4. Click "Render" in RStudio to make the slideshow
5. Practice! 10 minutes talk + 5 minutes questions

TIPS
- Less text per slide = better slide
- Every figure needs: axis labels + one sentence saying what it shows
- It is YOUR story: "we predicted... we found... we were surprised..."
- One fun slide about camp life at the end is a tradition!
-->

## Our question {.center}

<!-- One sentence. Big font. Why did it interest you? -->

*(example: "When does copying friends make a group smarter?")*

## The world we built

<!-- Explain your model LIKE A GAME:
     - what can agents choose?
     - what do they know?
     - what are the rules? (learning, copying)
     Use a drawing or diagram if you can! -->

- Agents choose between ___ slot machines
- They learn from rewards (learning rate $\alpha$)
- They can see what others chose ($\sigma$ = how much they copy)

## What we did

<!-- Your simulation experiment in 3 bullets:
     what did you vary? what did you measure? how many repetitions? -->

- We varied: ___
- We measured: ___
- We repeated each setting ___ times

## Our prediction

<!-- What did you THINK would happen, before running it?
     Being wrong is 100% okay - surprises are the best part of science -->

## Results

<!-- Put your main figure here! To include a saved figure:

![](../figs/our_result.png)

Or generate it live by putting your R code in a chunk
(change ```r to ```{r} to make it run):

```r
source("R/bandit_functions.R")
# ... your simulation + ggplot code ...
```
-->

## What it means

<!-- 2-3 bullets:
     - answer to your question
     - connection to real life (trends? rumours? markets?)
     - what you would try next with more time -->

## What we learned at RIKEN

<!-- The human side: lab visits, lunch discoveries, favourite moments.
     Photos welcome! -->

## Thank you! {.center}

<!-- names + one line each: "my favourite part was..." -->

**Questions welcome!**
